title: SaaS-Ready: The High-Performance Content Hub
description: Build a production-grade Content Hub (similar to a blog or documentation site) using Next.js 15. The project focuses on the 'Golden Path' of modern web dev: Server Components for speed, Client Components for interactivity, and Server Actions for data mutations.
requirements: A dynamic route `/articles/[slug]` that uses `generateStaticParams` to pre-render the top 10 most popular articles.
A 'Newsletter Subscription' form using a Server Action that validates input and updates a mock database.
A search bar that uses a Client Component and debounced input to filter a list of articles without page reloads.
Implementation of 'Streaming' using <Suspense> for a 'Related Articles' sidebar that loads slower than the main content.
A nested layout structure: Root Layout $ightarrow$ Blog Layout (Sidebar/Nav) $ightarrow$ Article Page.
Integration of a 'Table of Contents' that automatically generates links based on H2/H3 tags in the content.

milestones.title: The Static Shell
tasks: Build the Root and Blog layouts
Implement the main articles list page
Create the dynamic article page with basic data fetching

milestones.title: Interactivity & Mutation
tasks: Create the Subscribe form as a Client Component
Implement the Server Action to handle the subscription
Add a loading state using useFormStatus

milestones.title: Performance Optimization
tasks: Wrap the Related Articles section in Suspense with a skeleton

stretchGoals: Implement 'Incremental Static Regeneration' (ISR) to update articles without a full rebuild.
Add a 'Reading Progress Bar' that updates as the user scrolls.
Integrate a Markdown parser (like gray-matter) to load content from local .md files instead of a DB.

evaluationRubric.performance: Does the page maintain a perfect Lighthouse score? Are Server Components used where possible?
evaluationRubric.architecturalIntegrity: Is the boundary between Client and Server components clean? Is the folder structure logical?
evaluationRubric.ux: Is the streaming experience smooth? Does the form handle errors gracefully?
